metadata._verificationNotes: Internship titles, companies, and general program structures are based on real programs that historically exist. However, many entries share template descriptions and unverified deadlines/stipend amounts. Users should verify specific details (deadlines, pay, application links) directly on company career portals. The 'whyApply' field provides unique guidance per entry.
sources: Company career portals (Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Apple, etc.)
Government agencies (NASA, NIH, EPA, CDC, NSF, DoE)
Research institutions (NSF REU programs, DOE SULI, National Labs)
Consulting firms (McKinsey, BCG, Bain, Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG)
University research programs (UW, UMich, Stanford, etc.)

internships.title: Research Training Program (RTP)
internships.description: 4-week biomedical research program for rising juniors and seniors. Covers biochemistry, immunology, global health, infectious diseases. Labs include microscopy, gel electrophoresis, PCR, gene editing. In-person at Seattle Children's downtown.
internships.whyApply: One of the top biomedical research programs for high schoolers in the Pacific Northwest. Hands-on lab work with real researchers.

internships.title: Summer High School Internship Program (SHIP)
internships.stipend: Financial award upon completion + free ORCA transit card
internships.description: 8-week full-time cancer research internship for students between 11th-12th grade. Two weeks of lab safety training, then 6 weeks embedded in a research group. Includes research seminars, professional development workshops, and social activities.
internships.whyApply: Work alongside world-class cancer researchers at one of the nation's top cancer research centers. Strong pipeline for college applications.

internships.title: High School Intern Program
internships.description: Summer internship at ISB for high school students interested in systems biology, computational biology, and data science. Both unpaid service learning and some paid positions available. 40 hours/week.
internships.whyApply: ISB pioneered the field of systems biology. Exposure to cutting-edge computational and biological research.

internships.title: Whale Scout Youth Internship
internships.description: Environmental conservation program for high school-aged youth. No experience required. Focus on orca whale conservation, habitat restoration, and community engagement in the Puget Sound region.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on environmental work in one of the most biodiverse marine regions in the US. Great for students passionate about conservation.

internships.title: Career Quest Internship Program
internships.description: Seattle Public Schools program placing students with local organizations and businesses for career exploration. Earn high school credit while exploring careers. Multiple fields available depending on host organization placement.
internships.whyApply: Earn high school credit while getting real workplace experience. Wide variety of placement options across Seattle employers.

internships.title: NSF REU Site: Engineering & Computer Science
internships.description: NSF-funded Research Experiences for Undergraduates program in engineering and computer science. Work with faculty mentors on real research projects. Includes professional development workshops and research presentations.
internships.whyApply: Federally funded research experience with stipend, housing, and travel. Excellent for graduate school applications.

internships.title: UW Undergraduate Research Program
internships.description: UW connects undergraduates with faculty research mentors across all disciplines. Multiple pathways including Mary Gates Research Scholarships, directed research credits, and summer research programs.
internships.whyApply: Access to one of the top public research universities in the nation. Multiple funding mechanisms available.

internships.title: Research Internship in CS Labs
internships.description: Intensive research internship in computer science labs for students age 15+. Work alongside CS faculty and grad students on real research projects in AI, cybersecurity, robotics, and more.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on CS research experience at a top Texas research university. Strong for college applications in STEM fields.

internships.title: Clark Scholar Program
internships.description: Highly competitive 7-week research program for rising high school seniors. Students work one-on-one with faculty mentors on original research in any discipline. Includes weekly seminars and social activities.
internships.whyApply: One of the most prestigious high school research programs in Texas. Free room/board plus stipend. Extremely competitive (~4% acceptance).

internships.title: Junior Volunteer Program
internships.description: Hospital volunteer program for high school students ages 16+. Work in patient care areas, therapy departments, gift shops, and administrative offices. Minimum commitment varies by location.
internships.whyApply: Direct healthcare exposure in one of Texas's largest hospital systems. Great for pre-med students building clinical hours.

internships.title: SEES High School Summer Intern Program
internships.company: UT Center for Space Research / NASA
internships.description: STEM Enhancement in Earth Science program. Students work with NASA scientists, industry experts, and academics on earth science research. Covers satellite data analysis, field research, and computational modeling.
internships.whyApply: Work directly with NASA scientists. Incredible for college applications and genuine interest in earth/space science.

internships.title: Houston Methodist Summer Internship Program
internships.description: High school students work alongside scientists and doctors in regenerative medicine, cancer therapies, and cardiovascular health research labs.
internships.whyApply: One of the top-ranked hospitals in Texas. Direct exposure to cutting-edge medical research.

internships.title: SPARK Program
internships.description: 7-week hands-on research experience for San Diego County high schoolers in biological and chemical sciences. Students are matched with research mentors and work on real laboratory projects.
internships.whyApply: One of the world's largest independent biomedical research institutes. Genuine hands-on laboratory experience.

internships.title: A-LIFT Pre-College Summer Program
internships.description: Department of Energy national lab program for high school students. Research experience in physics, chemistry, environmental science, and computational science at one of the premier national laboratories.
internships.whyApply: Work at a world-class DOE national laboratory. Exceptional for students interested in physics and advanced STEM research.

internships.title: Summer Research Internship Program
internships.description: Cancer research internship for undergraduates at one of the nation's leading cancer treatment and research centers. Students work on projects in molecular biology, immunology, and clinical research.
internships.whyApply: City of Hope is an NCI-designated comprehensive cancer center. Excellent research exposure and mentorship.

internships.title: JPL Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Research internships at NASA JPL for college undergrads. Work on real space missions, robotics, AI, and planetary science projects alongside NASA engineers and scientists.
internships.whyApply: Work on actual NASA missions at the world-famous JPL. Highly competitive but transformative experience.

internships.title: Science Research Mentoring Program (SRMP)
internships.description: 10-month research program for NYC high school students. Conduct original research with museum scientists in fields like astrophysics, paleontology, genomics, and earth science. Includes science seminars and research presentations.
internships.whyApply: Work with world-renowned scientists at one of the most famous museums in the world. $2,500 stipend. Exceptional for college applications.

internships.title: Student Historian Program
internships.description: High school students conduct research on history topics, work with historian mentors, visit archives, lead gallery tours, and develop research skills at one of NYC's oldest museums.
internships.whyApply: Unique humanities research opportunity. Stand out for college applications with real archival research experience.

internships.title: NYC Youth Health Volunteer Program
internships.description: Youth Leadership Council and health program for students 16+. Conduct patient surveys, focus groups, and interviews. Professional development through internships at NYC's public hospital system.
internships.whyApply: Direct experience in the largest public hospital system in the US. Great for students interested in public health and health equity.

internships.title: RENEW-Midwest Summer Research Program
internships.description: 10-week summer research program in environmental and energy engineering. NSF-funded REU program hosted at University of Michigan in partnership with other universities.
internships.whyApply: NSF-funded program at one of the top public research universities. Free housing and stipend. Strong for grad school applications.

internships.title: Undergraduate Research Opportunity Program (UROP)
internships.description: UMich's flagship undergraduate research program pairs students with faculty mentors across all disciplines. First and second year students especially encouraged. Includes peer advising and research seminars.
internships.whyApply: One of the oldest and largest undergraduate research programs in the country. Open to all disciplines.

internships.title: NASA Internship Program (OSTEM)
internships.stipend: Paid based on academic level (~$2,400 for HS)
internships.description: Research internships at NASA's 10 field centers nationwide. Work on real missions in aerospace engineering, planetary science, robotics, AI, and more. In-person or virtual options available.
internships.whyApply: Work on actual NASA missions. Multiple locations and virtual options. Looks incredible on college and job applications.

internships.title: NIH Summer Internship Program (SIP)
internships.description: Biomedical and behavioral research internships at NIH campuses. Must be 18 by September 30 (or 17 with home within 40 miles of NIH). Work with leading researchers on health-related research projects.
internships.whyApply: The largest biomedical research agency in the world. Unmatched for students pursuing careers in medicine or biomedical research.

internships.title: NMNH Summer High School Internship
internships.company: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History
internships.description: High school internship (ages 15-18, grades 9-12) at the Smithsonian. Work with museum departments on science and non-science projects. Tues-Fri, 10am-4pm. Includes enrichment activities.
internships.whyApply: Work at the Smithsonian — one of the most recognized museums in the world. $5,600 stipend. Exceptional for college applications.

internships.title: DOE SULI Program
internships.description: Science Undergraduate Laboratory Internships at 17 DOE national labs across the US. Research in physics, chemistry, engineering, biology, environmental science, and computing. Includes travel reimbursement.
internships.whyApply: Access to world-class national laboratory facilities. $600/week stipend plus housing. Exceptional for graduate school applications.

internships.title: Summer Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Hospital volunteer program for high school students at UW Medicine facilities. Assist in patient care areas, therapy departments, and administrative offices. Minimum age 16.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at one of the top academic medical centers in the Pacific Northwest. Build clinical hours for pre-med applications.

internships.title: Boeing High School Internship
internships.description: Boeing offers select high school internship opportunities in aerospace engineering and manufacturing at their Puget Sound facilities. Students must be at least 16 years old.
internships.whyApply: The world's largest aerospace company, headquartered in the Seattle metro. Genuine aerospace engineering exposure.

internships.title: Washington State Opportunity Scholarship STEM Internship
internships.description: WSOS connects low/middle-income WA students with STEM and healthcare career pathways. Includes mentoring, professional development, and connections to internship opportunities statewide.
internships.whyApply: Comprehensive career support system for Washington state residents. Combines financial aid with career development.

internships.title: Woodland Park Zoo Conservation Internship
internships.description: Conservation and animal care internships for students interested in wildlife biology, zoo management, and environmental education. Work alongside zoo staff on conservation projects.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on wildlife conservation experience at a major metropolitan zoo. Great for students interested in biology or veterinary science.

internships.title: Stanford Institutes of Medicine Summer Research Program (SIMR)
internships.description: 8-week biomedical research program for high school juniors and seniors. Students work in Stanford faculty labs on real research projects in medicine, biology, and bioengineering.
internships.whyApply: Research at one of the most prestigious universities in the world. Extremely competitive but life-changing for accepted students.

internships.title: Rockefeller University Summer Science Research Program
internships.description: Highly competitive summer research program for rising seniors. Work one-on-one with Rockefeller University scientists on biomedical research. Includes lectures, workshops, and culminating research presentations.
internships.whyApply: Rockefeller has produced more Nobel laureates than almost any institution. Incredibly prestigious research experience.

internships.title: Memorial Sloan Kettering Human Oncology & Pathogenesis Program
internships.description: Summer research program for undergraduate students interested in cancer biology and biomedical research at one of the world's top cancer centers.
internships.whyApply: Work at the world's oldest and largest private cancer center. Unmatched for students pursuing oncology or biomedical research careers.

internships.title: Polygence Research Program
internships.description: 1-on-1 mentored research program matching high school students with PhD mentors in any field. Students complete an original research project and can publish in peer-reviewed journals. Fully remote.
internships.whyApply: Flexible remote program with PhD mentors. Good for students who can't access local programs. Note: this is a paid service, not free.

internships.title: Lumiere Research Scholar Program
internships.description: Research mentoring program pairing high school students with Harvard PhD/postdoc mentors for original research projects. Fully online. Students can work toward publication.
internships.whyApply: Access to Harvard-affiliated mentors from anywhere. Good for students in areas with limited local research programs. Note: this is a paid service.

internships.title: Open Science Quest at Allen Institute
internships.description: 3-week career intensive at the Allen Institute in South Lake Union. Students dive deep into cell biology, neuroscience, and immunology with world-class researchers. Includes orientation, daily programming 9am-4pm, and culminating project.
internships.whyApply: Work at a premier brain and cell science research institute. Unparalleled exposure to cutting-edge neuroscience and cell biology.

internships.title: Clean Water Ambassadors Internship
internships.description: 5-week paid summer internship for a cohort of 12 high school youth (16+). Learn about local environmental issues and solutions, then practice educating community members. Meet Mon-Fri 9am-3pm in downtown Seattle. Free public transit provided.
internships.whyApply: Paid environmental internship with strong community engagement component. Great for students passionate about water quality and environmental justice.

internships.title: Microsoft Discovery Program
internships.description: 4-week paid internship for graduating high school seniors living within 50 miles of Redmond, WA. Work in small teams on real projects spanning product development, software engineering, product management, and UX design. No prior technical experience required. Must be affiliated with a Microsoft-sponsored org (Computing for All, College Success Foundation, Rainier Scholars, TAF, UW STEM Upward Bound, or Washington MESA).
internships.whyApply: Hands-on tech experience at one of the world's largest tech companies. Great for college applications and exploring career paths in tech.

internships.title: Amazon Periscope Finance Internship
internships.description: 2-week career intensive at Amazon South Lake Union campus for current 10th grade students. Introduction to finance and accounting careers including Microsoft Excel training, day-in-the-life of a financial analyst, personal finance workshops, and a business case study capstone project. 30 students accepted. Earns 0.25 CTE credits plus ongoing Amazon leadership mentorship.
internships.whyApply: Get real exposure to corporate finance at Amazon with ongoing mentorship from Amazon leadership. Unique opportunity for 10th graders.

internships.title: T-Mobile Career Launch Web Developer Program
internships.description: Career Launch program for Seattle Public Schools high school juniors combining paid summer internships at T-Mobile with college coursework at Seattle Colleges. Students work toward a 59-credit full stack web developer certificate over 2 years, with two paid summer internships at T-Mobile headquarters.
internships.whyApply: Earn college credits and paid work experience simultaneously at a major tech company. Pipeline to a full stack developer career.

internships.title: Port of Seattle High School Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship across a variety of fields including aviation operations, maritime operations, governance, community engagement, and green jobs. Includes mentorship meetings, facility tours, LinkedIn 101, resume workshops, and professional development. Must be at least 16 years old.
internships.whyApply: Unique exposure to aviation and maritime industries at a major West Coast port. Paid with professional development programming.

internships.title: Seattle Youth Employment Program (SYEP)
internships.description: 6-8 week paid summer internship placing 250 youth (ages 16-24) in City departments, nonprofits, and private sector employers. Selected by lottery. Must live in Seattle or attend Seattle Public Schools/Seattle Colleges with household income at or below 80% AMI.
internships.whyApply: Great entry-level paid work experience with the City of Seattle. Builds professional skills and resume for college applications.

internships.title: Seattle Indian Health Board Public Health Internship
internships.description: Public health internship focused on American Indian/Alaska Native health. 20 hours/week for 8 weeks. Open to high school students ages 16-18 interested in public health. Runs June 29 - August 21, 2026.
internships.whyApply: Well-paid public health internship with unique focus on AI/AN health disparities. Strong addition to healthcare-focused college applications.

internships.title: ISB Paid Summer STEM Internship
internships.description: 8-week paid STEM internship for 11th graders at the Institute for Systems Biology. Work in a lab with professionals exploring computational biology, microbial interactions, and cancer research. Collaborate with mentors on ongoing research projects. July 1 - August 23.
internships.whyApply: Paid $5,000 stipend for cutting-edge computational biology research. ISB is a world leader in systems biology.

internships.title: High School Research Program (HSRP)
internships.description: Competitive 6-week program where students work alongside Brookhaven Lab scientific and engineering staff on hands-on research projects supporting the DOE mission. Covers physics, chemistry, biology, environmental science, and engineering. Commuter program, Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm. Concludes with poster session and oral presentations.
internships.whyApply: Work at a U.S. Department of Energy national laboratory alongside world-class scientists. Exceptional for college applications in any STEM field.

internships.title: Ellen Brenner Memorial Summer Internship
internships.description: High school juniors and seniors from Long Island work on mentored projects in CSHL Library and Archives related to the history of molecular biology. Two tracks available: Library Interns gain experience in science librarianship; Archives Interns work with collections documenting the history of CSHL and molecular biology including digitization, oral history, and cataloging.
internships.whyApply: Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory is one of the most storied biological research institutions in the world. Unique intersection of science and humanities.

internships.title: Aspirnaut Summer Research Internship
internships.company: University of Michigan Life Sciences Institute
internships.stipend: Housing, meals, and monthly stipend provided
internships.description: Immersive 6-week residential program where high school juniors join real research projects led by University of Michigan scientists. Students live on campus in double-occupancy housing, eat at campus dining, and receive a monthly stipend. Includes professional development, mentorship, college advising, and culminates in public research presentations.
internships.whyApply: Fully funded residential research at a top public university. Exceptional mentorship and direct research experience for college applications.

internships.title: UW Botanic Gardens Youth Summer Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship for teens with a commitment to community and equity. No previous experience in environmental issues required. Interns work alongside Botanic Gardens educators leading youth programming, maintaining plant collections, and supporting community engagement events across the Washington Park Arboretum and UW campus gardens.
internships.whyApply: Paid environmental internship at a major research university botanical garden. Great for students interested in ecology, conservation, or outdoor education.

internships.title: Seattle City Light College Internship Program
internships.description: Over 40 paid internship roles across data science, engineering, customer experience, accounting, environmental science, and more at Seattle's publicly owned electric utility. Summer interns typically work 40 hours/week embedded in real teams at one of the nation's greenest utilities. Positions posted in January each year.
internships.whyApply: 40+ paid roles at a municipal utility committed to 100% clean energy. Excellent government work experience with real project ownership.

internships.title: UW NOAA Fisheries Science Summer Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship where undergraduates work directly with a NOAA scientist to integrate mathematics and fisheries science. Based at the UW School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences in collaboration with NOAA's Northwest Fisheries Science Center. Students conduct original research on marine ecosystems, fish population modeling, or climate impacts on fisheries.
internships.whyApply: Work one-on-one with a NOAA research scientist. $6,400 stipend plus potential for published co-authorship. Outstanding for grad school applications.

internships.title: UW Teaching Tomorrow Internship
internships.description: 6-month internship (March-August) for Seattle Public Schools high school students age 16+. Interns work in early learning classrooms gaining hands-on experience with child development, curriculum planning, and classroom management. Program provides mentorship from UW education faculty and builds skills for careers in education, psychology, or social work.
internships.whyApply: Rare education-focused internship with UW faculty mentorship. Outstanding for students considering teaching, child psychology, or social work.

internships.title: Fred Hutch High School Explorers Program
internships.description: After-school program for 9th and 10th graders interested in biomedical science. Students visit Fred Hutch labs, meet researchers, learn about cancer biology and scientific careers. Distinct from the competitive SHIP summer internship — Explorers is designed as a pipeline to build interest and readiness for future research opportunities. Meets weekly during the school year.
internships.whyApply: Pipeline program at a world-class cancer research center. Builds foundation for competitive summer research internships like SHIP.

internships.title: Fred Hutch Pathways Undergraduate Research Program
internships.description: 10-week paid summer research internship for undergraduates from groups underrepresented in the sciences. Students are embedded in Fred Hutch research labs conducting independent projects in cancer biology, immunology, computational biology, or public health sciences. Includes professional development seminars, GRE prep, and graduate school advising.
internships.whyApply: Fully funded research at a top cancer center. Strong track record of placing alumni in graduate programs at elite institutions.

internships.title: UW EarthLab Undergraduate Summer Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship for UW undergraduates (Seattle, Tacoma, or Bothell campuses) focused on environmental and sustainability research. Interns work with interdisciplinary faculty on projects spanning climate science, environmental justice, urban ecology, and conservation. Includes cohort-based professional development and research presentations.
internships.whyApply: Interdisciplinary environmental research at UW with strong faculty mentorship. Excellent for students bridging science and policy.

internships.title: Port of Seattle College & Graduate Internship Program
internships.description: Paid internship for college and graduate students at the Port of Seattle, which operates Seattle-Tacoma International Airport and regional maritime facilities. Interns rotate through departments including aviation operations, maritime logistics, environmental programs, finance, engineering, IT, and community engagement. Includes mentorship, facility tours, and professional development workshops.
internships.whyApply: Work at one of the busiest airports and ports on the West Coast. Exceptional government career pipeline with diverse department rotations.

internships.title: City of Seattle Government Internships
internships.description: The City of Seattle offers paid internships across dozens of departments including transportation, parks, public utilities, IT, human services, planning, and public health. Most are part-time during the academic year and full-time during summer. Open to college students and recent graduates. Positions posted on the city's careers portal.
internships.whyApply: Broad range of paid government internships across all city departments. Outstanding for students interested in public service careers.

internships.title: UW INSIGHT High School Research Program
internships.company: UW Harborview Injury Prevention & Research Center
internships.description: 4-week summer research program for high school students entering grades 10-12. Students dive deep into injury and violence prevention research through hands-on investigations, data analysis, and mentored projects. Virtual format for 2026. Topics include traffic safety, firearm violence prevention, substance use, and health equity. Students are accepted on a rolling basis.
internships.whyApply: Research experience at UW's nationally recognized injury prevention center. Strong letter-of-rec potential for public health and pre-med applicants.

internships.title: Boeing Manufacturing Student Development Program
internships.description: Paid on-site manufacturing internship for high school students at Boeing factories in Everett, Renton, and Auburn. Roles include Assembly, Toolmaker, Test, Facilities, and Mechatronics. Program includes tailored hands-on training, factory floor experience, resume and interview workshops, networking with Boeing engineers, and career planning support. Distance bonus available for students 65+ miles from site.
internships.whyApply: Build real aircraft at one of the world's largest aerospace manufacturers. Paid with professional development and direct industry connections.

internships.title: ISB Summer Undergraduate Research Experiences (SURE)
internships.description: Paid 10-week summer research program for undergraduates at the Institute for Systems Biology. Students work in computational biology, cancer research, microbiome science, infectious disease, or aging research alongside ISB scientists. Includes professional development, research presentations, and access to ISB's cutting-edge multi-omics facilities.
internships.whyApply: Research at a pioneering systems biology institute. Ideal for students at the intersection of biology and computer science.

internships.title: SAM Teen Programs Internship
internships.description: Part-time (10 hrs/week) paid internship supporting the Education team in developing intergenerational programming for youth and families. Runs October through June (~36 weeks). Designed to further racial equity and diversity objectives within SAM. No prior museum experience required.
internships.whyApply: Paid museum internship at one of the Pacific Northwest's premier art museums. No prior experience needed — great for students exploring arts careers.

internships.title: KEXP Youth Radio Program
internships.description: Free educational program teaching young people music curation and radio broadcasting. Work alongside KEXP DJs to develop skills in music selection, board operation, digital and physical music library management, and on-air broadcasting. Must live in or around the Seattle-Tacoma metro area.
internships.whyApply: Unique opportunity to learn radio broadcasting at KEXP, one of the most respected independent radio stations in the world. Great for media-interested students.

internships.title: KEXP 2026 Internships
internships.description: 6-month paid internship (May-November, ~18 hrs/week, 450 total hours) at KEXP radio. Tracks include Communications & Audience Engagement, Community Events, Latin American Content, and Library & Archives. Seeks applicants from historically underrepresented communities at the beginning of their career.
internships.whyApply: Paid, hands-on media experience at a world-renowned independent radio station. Multiple tracks let you match your interests.

internships.title: 5th Avenue Theatre Rising Star Project
internships.description: Tuition-free youth theater program for WA teens ages 14-19. ~200 hours of rehearsal with professional mentors. Tracks include performance, theater design/technology, artistic leadership, and arts administration. Students produce a full-scale musical alongside theater professionals.
internships.whyApply: Professional-level theater experience at one of Seattle's top venues. Completely free with multiple career tracks beyond just performing.

internships.title: Village Theatre KIDSTAGE Internship
internships.stipend: Small cost-reimbursement stipend for some roles
internships.description: Summer theater internship at Village Theatre's KIDSTAGE program. Roles in classroom instruction, stage management, design, costumes, sound, lights, and directing. The Summer Independent Production (SIP) is tuition-free and puts students in charge of every aspect of production. Ages 18+ (younger by permission).
internships.whyApply: Hands-on theater production experience on Seattle's Eastside. SIP program gives students rare autonomy over full productions.

internships.title: FareStart Youth Barista & Customer Service Program
internships.description: 8-week workforce training program for youth ages 15.5-24. Job Readiness pathway: 4 weeks online + 4 weeks paid in-person training at FareStart Cafe learning espresso, register, customer service. Education pathway in partnership with Seattle Public Schools earns credit and stipend. 1:1 mentorship ratio.
internships.whyApply: Structured workforce training with real cafe experience and 1:1 mentorship. Good for students who need flexible entry points into the workforce.

internships.title: YMCA WAGES Youth Employment Program
internships.description: 10-week paid cohort program for ages 17-24. ~45 hours of classroom soft skills training + ~100 hours of internship placement at organizations like Burien CARES, College Nannies, Farmer's Market, and more. Develops employment readiness skills.
internships.whyApply: Paid $20.97/hr with built-in soft skills training. Great first job experience with YMCA's support network.

internships.title: YWCA Youth Employment Services
internships.description: Career and education assistance for young adults ages 15-24. Includes job coaching, college planning, internship referrals, resume/cover letter help, interview prep, and short-term training programs including Customer Service Training in partnership with Starbucks.
internships.whyApply: Comprehensive career support with Starbucks partnership. Good for students who need wraparound job-readiness support.

internships.title: Pierce County Summer Internship Program
internships.stipend: Above minimum wage (rate varies by department)
internships.description: 12-week paid summer internship (June-August) across Pierce County government departments. Real, meaningful work — not routine tasks. Assigned a mentor, free ORCA Card for transit, some hybrid/remote options. Departments include public works, IT, parks, human services, and more.
internships.whyApply: Paid government internship with real project work and mentorship. Free ORCA Card and potential hybrid schedule.

internships.title: Tacoma Jobs 253 Internship Program
internships.description: Paid internship program connecting Tacoma HS students (ages 14-18) with local businesses for 40+ hours of work experience per session. 5 sessions per year. Earn stipend + high school credit. Must complete reflection form, resume, and financial literacy certificate. Up to 3 experiences total.
internships.whyApply: Accessible paid program for Tacoma students with rolling entry points throughout the year. Builds resume and earns credit.

internships.title: City of Kirkland Parks & Community Services Internship
internships.description: Summer paid internship for high school students in Kirkland Parks & Community Services. 10-20 hrs/week in recreation, parks management, Green Kirkland Partnership, administration, or human services. Tasks include outdoor programs, research, photo/video editing, and promotional materials.
internships.whyApply: Paid local government experience on the Eastside. Flexible hours and diverse assignment areas from parks to media.

internships.title: Washington State Legislature Page Program
internships.description: 1-week paid program for youth ages 14-17 during the legislative session (Jan-Apr). Pages assist on the chamber floor, make deliveries, support member offices, and attend Page School learning about state government. Sponsored by a legislator from your district. House pays $65/day, Senate $67/day.
internships.whyApply: See state government from the inside. Work on the chamber floor alongside legislators. Paid stipend and excellent civics education.

internships.title: MultiCare M.A.S.H. Camp
internships.description: Free 5-day healthcare career exploration camp for high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors. Hands-on station rotations, mock codes, job shadows, and college visits. Learn about nursing, pharmacy, radiology, physical therapy, and allied health. Must be in Pierce, south King, Kitsap, or north Thurston counties.
internships.whyApply: Free immersive healthcare experience at a major regional health system. Over 20 years of inspiring future healthcare professionals.

internships.title: MultiCare Summer Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer program at MultiCare hospitals. Must be at least 16 years old and commit to 100+ hours of service. After 60 volunteer hours, eligible for job shadowing in clinical departments. Applications open Feb 1 - Apr 15.
internships.whyApply: Earn clinical volunteer hours at a major hospital system, then unlock job shadowing. Strong foundation for pre-med applications.

internships.title: Museum of Glass Curator High Program
internships.description: Curatorial intensive for Tacoma high school students in partnership with Jobs 253. Work with every department at Museum of Glass to conceive, develop, and install an exhibition in the Grand Hall. Learn museum organizational structure and gain professional development.
internships.whyApply: Rare curatorial experience for high schoolers. Curate a real exhibition at a world-class museum. Paid through Jobs 253.

internships.title: King County Parks Youth Conservation Corps (YCC)
internships.description: 6-week paid summer internship for teens entering grades 10-12. Learn about public lands, environmental justice, and environmental careers. Work Mon-Thu (22 hrs/week), Thursdays virtual. Sites at White Center or Skyway. Must be at least 15 by program start and a King County student.
internships.whyApply: Paid conservation work with environmental justice focus. Hands-on public lands experience for environmental science college applications.

internships.title: Northwest Youth Corps Sound to Summit
internships.description: 5-week day conservation crew program for youth ages 15-18 in Tacoma, Olympia, and Lakewood areas. Complete service projects on public lands averaging 6.5 hours/day of work + 1 hour of education. Earn a stipend and high school credit.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on conservation work on public lands with an education component. Build outdoor skills while earning a stipend.

internships.title: Everett Career Link Summer Internship
internships.company: Everett Public Schools / Snohomish STEM / City of Everett
internships.description: 90-hour summer internship (5-6 weeks, July-August) connecting Everett HS sophomores/juniors with employers including Kaiser Permanente and Providence. Partnership between Everett Public Schools, Snohomish STEM, and City of Everett. Earns 0.5 CTE/Elective credit.
internships.whyApply: Structured employer placements with real businesses in Snohomish County. Earn high school credit while exploring careers.

internships.title: Overlake Medical Center Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer program for teens at Overlake Medical Center in Bellevue. Supports Nursing Assistant Certified (NAC) observation hours for high school students in the healthcare track. Application window opens January. Contact 425-688-5921.
internships.whyApply: Clinical volunteer experience at a top-rated Eastside hospital. Supports NAC certification pathway.

internships.title: Hilltop Artists Youth Glassblowing Program
internships.description: Free tuition glassblowing and flameworking program for youth ages 12-20 in the Tacoma area. Team-based studio sessions at the Hilltop Artists studio and Jason Lee Middle School hotshop. No experience required. Offers after-school, summer intensive, and advanced track programs.
internships.whyApply: Nationally recognized free youth glass art program in Tacoma. Unique creative skill-building with mentorship from professional artists. Great for arts-focused college applications.

internships.title: YA4-H! Youth Advocates for Health
internships.description: Paid youth internship through WSU Spokane County Extension and 4-H for teens ages 13-18. Youth advocates lead health education workshops in their communities, focusing on mental health, nutrition, and substance prevention. Includes leadership training and public speaking development.
internships.whyApply: Paid public health leadership role through WSU Extension. Builds community health education skills and strengthens college apps with real advocacy experience. One of few paid HS opportunities in Spokane.

internships.title: TIPS - Teens in Public Service
internships.description: Paid summer internship placing teens ages 15-19 at nonprofit organizations across the Puget Sound region. Interns complete 160 hours of meaningful service work over 8 weeks while earning $21.30/hour. Includes professional development workshops, community building, and exposure to nonprofit careers.
internships.whyApply: One of the highest-paying HS internships in WA at $21.30/hr. Real nonprofit work experience across diverse organizations. Strong community impact for college essays.

internships.title: Evergreen Goodwill Youth Maritime Program
internships.description: Two-year paid program for HS juniors combining maritime career training with college-level coursework. Includes Saturday workshops, up to 4 quarters of maritime classes at area colleges, a summer internship, and career navigation services. Students learn small-boat sailing, boat maintenance, repair, and earn industry certifications (CPR, OSHA, Food Handlers). Over $5,000 in total stipends available.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid maritime career pathway for HS students. Combines hands-on sailing and boat work with college credits and industry certifications. Strong stipend structure with $5,000+ total earning potential.

internships.title: Lake Roosevelt NPS Youth Conservation Corps
internships.description: Paid summer conservation program at Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area. Up to 9 enrollees work at Spring Canyon, Fort Spokane, and Kettle Falls on trail maintenance, habitat restoration, and park operations. Ages 15-18. Runs June 15 through August 21. Non-residential — participants must provide own transportation.
internships.whyApply: Work for the National Park Service in scenic eastern Washington. Real conservation experience with federal employment on your resume. One of the only paid NPS youth programs in WA state.

internships.title: SCA Seattle Urban Green Program
internships.description: Paid summer conservation crew positions for youth ages 14-18 in partnership with Seattle Parks and Recreation. Crews work 35 hours per week on trail building, habitat restoration, urban forestry, and environmental education projects across Seattle parks. July through August. No prior experience required.
internships.whyApply: Get paid to do conservation work in Seattle parks. Nationally recognized SCA credential on your resume. Great for students interested in environmental careers. Ages 14+ makes it accessible to younger high schoolers.

internships.title: Pacific Science Center Science Educators in Training
internships.description: Summer volunteer program for teens at Pacific Science Center's Camps for Curious Minds. Science Educators in Training help teach campers about topics from robotics to ecology to cooking science across 8 Puget Sound locations. Gain leadership skills, service learning hours, and STEM teaching experience. Ages 14-18.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on STEM teaching experience at Seattle's premier science museum. Volunteer across 8 Puget Sound locations. Excellent for college apps showing science communication and leadership.

internships.title: Urban Fresh Food Collective Urban Innovators Internship
internships.description: Paid youth internship at Marra Farm in South Park/Duwamish Valley. Ages 13-24. Combines farming and food justice education with community art and barista training. Youth learn sustainable agriculture, food sovereignty, espresso skills, and creative arts. Community-focused program addressing food access in the Duwamish Valley.
internships.whyApply: Unique paid internship combining urban farming, food justice, art, and barista skills. Real community impact in the Duwamish Valley. Stipend up to $2,100. Great for students interested in food systems and social justice.

internships.title: WA State Library Teen Internship Grant Program
internships.stipend: Varies by library (up to $5,000 grant per library)
internships.description: Paid summer internship program placing teens at public and tribal libraries across Washington state. Each intern works 100-300 hours over 6-10 weeks with a library staff mentor. Projects include collection development, programming, displays, summer reading, and a self-directed interest project. Statewide program with positions at participating libraries.
internships.whyApply: Paid library internship with mentorship and a self-directed project. Available statewide at public and tribal libraries. Builds research, communication, and professional skills.

internships.title: Museum of Flight Aeronautical Explorations
internships.description: Free three-week summer CTE program for WA state rising 9th-12th graders at the Museum of Flight. Explore aviation careers including air traffic control, airline dispatch, professional pilot, airport management, and unmanned aerial systems. Includes flight simulation, drone operation, and meetings with industry professionals. Earns 0.5 HS CTE credits. June 29 - July 17.
internships.whyApply: Completely free aviation career exploration at the Museum of Flight. Earn CTE credit on your transcript. Hands-on flight simulation and drone operation. Excellent for students interested in aerospace, piloting, or aviation management.

internships.title: City of Bellevue Well-KEPT Program
internships.description: Paid summer youth employment program through Bellevue Parks & Community Services. Ages 14-18 (middle and high school students). Work on forest enhancement, wetland restoration, and trail construction across Bellevue's park system. Combines hands-on conservation work with environmental education and career development training. 8 weeks, Mon-Fri 9am-3:30pm.
internships.whyApply: Paid city employment doing real conservation work in Bellevue parks. Environmental education plus career development training. Great for Eastside students interested in environmental careers.

internships.title: PNNL High School Research Internship
internships.description: Research and business internships at Pacific Northwest National Laboratory for high school students. Work alongside scientists and engineers on real research projects in computational biology, environmental science, cybersecurity, and more. Academic year: up to 4 hrs/day. Summer: full-time. Can earn school elective credits. Must be 18+ for lab spaces; business track available for younger students.
internships.whyApply: Work at a Department of Energy national laboratory — one of the most prestigious research internships available to WA high school students. Real research experience with mentorship from professional scientists. Located in Richland (Tri-Cities).

internships.title: Vancouver Public Schools Career Connected Learning Internship
internships.description: Paid internship program placing juniors and seniors (ages 16-18) at local businesses across Vancouver, WA. Interns receive a stipend upon completion plus funding for fuel, interview clothing, and worksite supplies. Includes mentoring, mock interviews, and career exploration. Youth apprenticeship track also available for manufacturing careers.
internships.whyApply: One of the only structured paid HS internship programs in Vancouver, WA. Stipend plus support for professional supplies and transportation. Good for students interested in manufacturing and trades careers.

internships.title: TeamChild Youth Advisory Board
internships.description: Youth advocacy board for ages 14-24 at TeamChild, a nonprofit providing free legal services to system-involved youth. Board members use lived experience to advocate for policy change through storytelling, education, and legislative reform. Available in King, Pierce, Yakima, and Spokane counties. Participants shape hiring decisions, communications strategy, and fundraising.
internships.whyApply: Rare youth legal advocacy opportunity in WA. Work alongside civil legal aid attorneys on real policy reform. Build public speaking, advocacy, and policy skills directly relevant to law school applications.

internships.title: Seattle Youth Traffic Court Volunteer
internships.description: High school students serve as judges, prosecutors, defense attorneys, jurors, and bailiffs in real traffic court hearings for teen drivers. Partnership between Seattle Municipal Court, Seattle University School of Law, Garfield HS, and Seattle Police. Law school staff and students mentor and train HS volunteers. Court meets first Monday of each month, September through June.
internships.whyApply: Handle real court cases as a teen judge or attorney. Mentored by Seattle University law students. One of the only programs in WA where HS students participate in actual legal proceedings.

internships.title: East King County Youth Traffic Court
internships.description: Student-led restorative justice program handling real traffic cases for 16-17 year old drivers in East King County. HS student volunteers serve as panel members questioning respondents and determining case dispositions. Meets twice monthly on Tuesdays (except June/July): first Tuesday for training, second for hearings. Open to any East King County high school student.
internships.whyApply: Handle real legal cases through restorative justice on the Eastside. Open to all East KC high school students (not limited to one school). Builds legal reasoning, compassionate questioning, and decision-making skills.

internships.title: Ballard HS Academy of Finance Internship
internships.company: NAF Academy of Finance / Seattle Public Schools
internships.description: Paid summer internship between junior and senior year through Ballard High School's NAF Academy of Finance. Students are placed at partner firms including K&L Gates, KeyBank, KPMG, and Marriott. Multi-year program includes accounting, finance, entrepreneurship courses, UW Foster School mentoring, and a senior-year trip to Wall Street in NYC. NAFTrack certification upon completion.
internships.whyApply: One of the only paid finance internships for WA high schoolers. NAFTrack certification provides priority interviewing at major firms. UW Foster School mentoring and Wall Street trip included. 1,400+ Core Plus graduates hired at partner companies.

internships.title: ACAP Seattle Accounting Residency Program
internships.description: Free summer residency week for underrepresented WA high school students interested in accounting and business careers. Includes intensive accounting education, career exploration, mentorship from accounting professionals, and scholarship opportunities. Since 1980, ACAP has focused on increasing diversity in the accounting profession.
internships.whyApply: Free intensive accounting career exploration. Scholarship opportunities and professional mentoring. Since 1980 — strong alumni network in Seattle accounting firms. Focus on underrepresented students.

internships.title: YEOC - Young Executives in Our Community
internships.description: Free 9-month college readiness program at UW Foster School of Business for first-generation and/or economically disadvantaged HS juniors and seniors statewide. Monthly Saturday sessions on UW campus covering college preparation, business lectures, professional development, and mentorship. Serves 160+ students from 73+ WA schools annually.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to UW Foster School of Business faculty and resources. Free program for first-gen and economically disadvantaged students. Strong college admissions support and business career preparation. Open to all WA schools.

internships.title: AJAC Youth Apprenticeship - Production Technician
internships.description: Registered 2,000-hour youth apprenticeship for HS juniors and seniors in advanced manufacturing. Combines paid on-the-job training with college-level coursework. Learn CNC machining, manual machining, robotics, production engineering, and fabrication. Earn journey-level certification, college credits, and HS diploma. Available through 13 school districts across WA state.
internships.whyApply: Earn ~$32K while getting a nationally-recognized manufacturing certification. 178+ youth hired through this program. One of WA's only registered youth apprenticeships in advanced manufacturing. Available across 13 school districts statewide.

internships.title: Boeing Core Plus Aerospace Student Development Program
internships.description: Paid summer manufacturing program at Boeing factories in Everett, Renton, and Auburn for WA high school students enrolled in Core Plus Aerospace or aligned manufacturing programs. Roles include Assembly, Toolmaker, Test, Facilities, and Mechatronics. Taught by experienced Boeing trainers in mechanical and electrical assembly. Distance bonus available for students 65+ miles from site.
internships.whyApply: Work on-site at Boeing factories alongside professional trainers. One of the most prestigious manufacturing programs for WA high schoolers. 1,400+ Core Plus graduates hired by Boeing since 2015. Direct pathway to aerospace manufacturing careers.

internships.title: Pierce County Skills Center Construction Trades Pre-Apprenticeship
internships.description: State-approved pre-apprenticeship program for juniors and seniors from 10 Pierce County school districts. Covers carpentry, electrical, welding, plumbing, masonry, HVAC, boilermaking, and roofing. Taught by industry professionals in state-of-the-art facilities. Students attend home school mornings, PCSC afternoons. Free tuition. Graduates receive preferred entry into union apprenticeship programs.
internships.whyApply: Free state-approved pre-apprenticeship covering 8+ construction trades. Preferred entry into union apprenticeships statewide. Industry-certified instructors. Available to students across 10 Pierce County school districts.

internships.title: Whatcom County Teen Court
internships.company: Northwest Youth Services / Whatcom County Superior Court
internships.description: Juvenile diversion program since 1998 where HS students serve as jurors, advocates, bailiffs, and clerks in real misdemeanor cases. Volunteer attorneys serve as judges and mentors. Meets 2nd Wednesday of each month. Won Liberty Bell Award from Whatcom County Bar Association and Ken Gass Founders Award. Partnership between Superior Court and Northwest Youth Services.
internships.whyApply: Handle real misdemeanor cases in Bellingham. Award-winning program mentored by volunteer attorneys. One of the longest-running teen courts in WA (since 1998). Only structured legal program in Whatcom County for HS students.

internships.title: Clallam County Youth Court
internships.description: One of only three courts in Washington State entirely run by youth. Grades 7-12 serve as jurors, defense advocates, and prosecutors handling misdemeanor and gross misdemeanor cases. Volunteers trained locally through the Prosecutor's Office and Juvenile Services, plus annual training at Seattle University Law School. Court of accountability founded on teen capability.
internships.whyApply: One of only 3 entirely youth-run courts in Washington State. Annual training at Seattle University Law School. Handle real criminal cases as student attorneys and jurors. Rare legal experience on the Olympic Peninsula.

internships.title: WANIC Aerospace Manufacturing Program
internships.description: Full-year 3-period skills center class at Liberty High School for juniors and seniors. Hands-on aerospace manufacturing including materials science, applied physics, electrical systems, hydraulics, and sheet metal fabrication. Earns 3 HS credits (CTE/ELA, Lab Science, Math). Free tuition. Serves 7 Eastside school districts: Bellevue, Issaquah, Lake Washington, Mercer Island, Northshore, Riverview, Snoqualmie Valley.
internships.whyApply: Free aerospace manufacturing training serving 7 Eastside school districts. Earn 3 HS credits including lab science and math. Hands-on advanced shop work in materials science and fabrication. Direct pathway to aerospace careers averaging $58K-$156K.

internships.title: Spokane Valley Tech Advanced Manufacturing
internships.description: Year-long advanced manufacturing program for HS juniors and seniors. 2.5 hours per day learning industrial safety, customer-focused design, precision measurement, sheet metal fabrication, welding, 3D printing, laser cutting, and manual machining. Includes Manufacturing Connect program with tours of 24+ local manufacturers including Kaiser Aluminum. Core Plus Aerospace aligned. Central Valley School District.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on manufacturing training in eastern WA with connections to 24+ local employers through Manufacturing Connect. Learn welding, machining, 3D printing, and laser cutting. Core Plus Aerospace aligned for Boeing pathway. Only advanced manufacturing program in greater Spokane area.

internships.title: Tri-Tech Skills Center Welding Technology
internships.description: Welding technology program for juniors and seniors from 8 Tri-Cities school districts (Columbia, Finley, Kennewick, Kiona-Benton, North Franklin, Pasco, Prosser, Richland). Free tuition. Students attend Tri-Tech for half day, home school for other half. Earn high school and college credits plus industry certifications. State-of-the-art welding facilities with instruction by industry professionals.
internships.whyApply: Free welding certification program serving 8 Tri-Cities school districts. Industry-certified instructors and state-of-the-art facilities. Earn both HS and college credits. Only welding skills center program in the Tri-Cities region.

internships.title: TiE Young Entrepreneurs (TYE) Seattle
internships.description: Year-long entrepreneurship program for grades 9-12. Students form 4-person teams to launch real startups using Lean Startup methodology. Curriculum covers ideation, design thinking, customer empathy, prototyping, business modeling, marketing, and finance. Mentored by local entrepreneurs and venture capitalists. Alternate Thursday evenings Oct-Apr (hybrid). Finals in May, global competition in June. 50 seats, first-come first-served. Fee: $499 (financial aid available).
internships.whyApply: Build a real startup with mentoring from VCs and entrepreneurs. Compete locally and globally for cash prizes up to $10,000. Learn business, finance, and marketing through hands-on experience. Financial aid available.

internships.title: Seattle Foundation Youth Grantmaking Board
internships.description: Youth philanthropy board for King County HS sophomores, juniors, and seniors. Members lead the full grantmaking process: identify community issues with field experts, develop requests for proposals, evaluate nonprofit applications, conduct site visits, and make $5,000-$15,000 in funding recommendations to the Seattle Foundation Board of Trustees. Free to participate. Learn nonprofit finance, community impact assessment, and strategic giving.
internships.whyApply: Make real grant decisions affecting Seattle nonprofits. Learn nonprofit finance and community impact assessment at one of WA's largest foundations. Unique leadership credential for college applications in business, finance, or public policy.

internships.title: Careers in Science (CiS) Intern Program
internships.description: Multi-year paid internship for SF high school students underrepresented in STEM. Interns conduct fieldwork, facilitate museum learning experiences, and attend bimonthly trainings covering science communication and career development. Must be 9th-10th grader in SFUSD with 2.5+ GPA.
internships.duration: Year-round (Saturdays during school, weekdays in summer)
internships.whyApply: Prestigious multi-year program at a world-class science museum with real fieldwork and STEM career exposure.

internships.title: UCSF SEP High School Intern Program
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer internship where SF high school rising seniors conduct biomedical research at UCSF labs. Students are matched with scientist mentors in areas like neuroscience, immunology, cancer, and molecular biology. Must attend SFUSD or SF charter school.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on biomedical research at a top-tier university hospital with $4,500 stipend.

internships.title: California Science Center Community Teen Interns
internships.description: Paid internship for LA-area high school students (grades 10-12) who live within 3 miles of the Science Center. Interns work in the Education Department, delivering hands-on science activities to museum guests. 7 hrs/week during school year, up to 35 hrs/week in summer. Must maintain 2.0+ GPA.
internships.whyApply: Long-running paid program at LA's premier science museum with hands-on teaching experience.

internships.title: USC Unruh Institute High School Summer Internship
internships.company: USC Dornsife Center for the Political Future
internships.description: Online summer internship for high school students interested in politics and environmental policy. In partnership with USC Wrigley Institute, interns analyze legislation and complete a policy memo and legislative process analysis. Completed work is published online with recognition as USC Research Intern.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious USC-affiliated political research experience with published work — strong for college apps.

internships.title: Enterprise for Youth Paid Internships
internships.description: Paid internships ($20/hr) for SF youth ages 14-21 across sectors including healthcare, technology, art/design, financial services, legal services, government, nonprofit, and environmental stewardship. 75-hour placements with 12 hrs/week, plus weekly professional development workshops and job coaching.
internships.whyApply: Excellent paid program across many industries with built-in career coaching and professional development.

internships.title: Sacramento Thousand Strong Internship
internships.description: Mayor's workforce initiative providing 300 hours of paid work experience plus 40 hours of workplace basics training for Sacramento high school students. Partners with school districts, community organizations, and businesses. Must be 16+ and enrolled in high school with valid work permit.
internships.whyApply: Substantial paid work experience with professional training — backed by the City of Sacramento.

internships.title: HIRE LA's Youth Summer Employment
internships.description: LA's premier youth employment program for ages 14-24. Participants earn $15/hr while gaining 120 hours of work experience in virtual and in-person roles across city departments and partner organizations. Includes mentorship and career readiness training.
internships.whyApply: Massive city-backed program with thousands of placements across diverse industries in LA.

internships.title: Salk Institute Heithoff-Brody High School Scholars
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer research internship at the world-renowned Salk Institute. High school students work 30-40 hours/week alongside Salk scientists, performing original research and building STEM career skills. Highly competitive.
internships.whyApply: World-class research institute — one of the most prestigious HS science internships in California.

internships.title: UCSD OPALS High School Research Internship
internships.description: UCSD's OPALS program empowers pre-college students through hands-on STEM research. Students work with university researchers on authentic engineering and science projects during the summer. Open to high school students; competitive admission.
internships.whyApply: Authentic university research experience at UC San Diego — strong for engineering-bound students.

internships.title: SF Project Pull Summer Internship
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer internship placing SF high school students (sophomores-seniors) in city departments including SFPUC. Interns learn about careers in architecture, business, engineering, and science. 20 hrs/week at $19.25/hr. Must live or attend school in SF.
internships.whyApply: Well-paid city internship with exposure to engineering, architecture, and public infrastructure careers.

internships.title: California Innocence Project HS Internship
internships.description: Legal internship for high school students at California Western School of Law working on wrongful conviction cases. Interns assist attorneys with case reviews, legal research, document organization, and public education. 15-35 hrs/week for minimum 10 weeks. In-person only.
internships.whyApply: Rare high school law internship working on real wrongful conviction cases — exceptional for law-bound students.

internships.title: KP Launch High School Summer Internship
internships.description: Seven-week paid summer internship introducing underrepresented high school students to healthcare careers across Northern California Kaiser facilities. Interns work full-time (M-F, 8am-5pm) in various hospital and administrative departments. Open to all NorCal high school students.
internships.whyApply: Major healthcare system internship with real hospital exposure — great for aspiring medical professionals.

internships.title: SF YouthWorks City Government Internship
internships.description: Paid after-school and summer internship placing SF 11th-12th graders in 30+ city government departments. Interns earn $19.18/hr for 6-10 hours/week during the school year. Over 20 years of operation providing public service career exposure.
internships.whyApply: Paid government internship across 30+ SF city departments — excellent for public service careers.

internships.title: Community Teen Interns Program
internships.description: Paid job for LA high school students (grades 10-12) in the Science Center's neighboring communities. Interns facilitate hands-on science activities, lead exhibit tours, and develop science communication skills while earning income.
internships.whyApply: Paid STEM job at a major LA museum — great for building science communication and public engagement skills.

internships.title: UCSF High School Intern Program
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer program where SF high school juniors conduct biomedical research in UCSF labs. Students work alongside scientists on real research projects in areas like cancer biology, neuroscience, and immunology.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious paid biomedical research experience at one of the nation's top medical schools.

internships.title: Scripps Health Summer High School Exploration Program
internships.description: Five-week summer internship introducing high school students to healthcare careers. Students shadow clinicians, attend workshops on medical specialties, and gain exposure to hospital operations across Scripps Health facilities.
internships.whyApply: Direct hospital exposure at one of San Diego's premier health systems — excellent for aspiring healthcare professionals.

internships.title: FACES for the Future Coalition — Sacramento
internships.description: Health career pipeline program for juniors and seniors at partner Sacramento high schools. Includes clinical internships, college preparation, health career workshops, leadership development, and wellness supports. Feeds into healthcare career pathways.
internships.whyApply: Comprehensive health career pipeline with mentorship, internships, and college prep — particularly strong for underrepresented students.

internships.title: California Judicial Branch Student Internship
internships.description: Unpaid internship at the policymaking body of California's court system. Students work in various divisions gaining experience in court administration, legal research, and judicial policy. Available during fall, spring, and summer sessions.
internships.whyApply: Rare opportunity to work inside California's court system — outstanding for students interested in law or public policy.

internships.title: California Attorney General Student Internship
internships.description: Internship within the California Attorney General's office providing exposure to criminal justice, civil law, consumer protection, and public interest law. Students assist with legal research, case preparation, and policy analysis.
internships.whyApply: Work directly in the state's top law enforcement office — unmatched exposure to state-level legal practice.

internships.title: YMCA Youth and Government — California
internships.description: Premier civic engagement program where 1,300+ California high school and middle school students participate in Model Legislature & Court. Students draft bills, argue cases, debate policy, and travel to the State Capitol in Sacramento for a multi-day conference.
internships.whyApply: California's largest youth civic program — 75+ year tradition of training future leaders in real legislative processes.

internships.title: Young Legislators Program
internships.description: Summer program for high school students (grades 9-12) offered by individual Assembly Members. Students learn about legislation, community issues, and government operations through seminars and a trip to the State Capitol.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to California state government from the inside — networking with elected officials and legislative staff.

internships.title: Bank of America Student Leaders Program — California
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer internship for high school juniors and seniors. Students work with local nonprofit partners on community development projects while building financial literacy, leadership, and professional skills. Also includes a Washington DC leadership summit.
internships.whyApply: Top-tier paid program combining finance exposure with nonprofit leadership — plus a national leadership summit in DC.

internships.title: Junior Achievement of Northern California
internships.description: Semester-long financial literacy and entrepreneurship courses for high school students in Northern California. Through school partnerships, students learn budgeting, investing, business plan creation, and workforce readiness skills from business mentors.
internships.whyApply: Free financial literacy program with real business mentors — excellent foundation for aspiring entrepreneurs and finance professionals.

internships.title: Getty Teen Gallery Guides
internships.description: Paid summer program for LA County high school students at the Getty Center and Getty Villa. Teen guides develop skills in art interpretation, public speaking, and visitor engagement while leading gallery experiences for museum visitors.
internships.whyApply: Paid arts internship at one of the world's most renowned museums — builds public speaking and cultural literacy skills.

internships.title: MOCA Teen Program
internships.company: Museum of Contemporary Art Los Angeles
internships.description: Paid academic-year internship for LA high school juniors and seniors. Work directly with museum staff and local artists on contemporary art projects, including planning Teen Night events and managing social media content for the museum.
internships.whyApply: Get paid to work with professional artists and curators at LA's premier contemporary art museum.

internships.title: Annenberg Youth Academy for Media and Civic Engagement
internships.description: Intensive three-week summer program selecting 26 high school students for training in multimedia production, journalism, interviewing, writing, public speech, and ethnography. Students produce original media projects addressing civic issues.
internships.whyApply: Highly selective media program at USC's world-class journalism school — only 26 students selected annually.

internships.title: Stanford Young Investigators Program
internships.description: Summer research internship for Bay Area high school students (within 25 miles of campus). Students work with Stanford researchers on sustainability science including marine fossil evolution, soil ecosystem health, fire management, water chemistry, and climate data analysis.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on environmental research at Stanford — one of the most prestigious sustainability research programs for high schoolers.

internships.title: Crystal Cove Conservancy Fire Ecology Internship
internships.description: Hands-on field internship for high school students studying fire ecology in coastal Southern California. Includes Saturday field days at Crystal Cove State Park and weekly virtual sessions covering wildfire science, native plant restoration, and ecological monitoring.
internships.whyApply: Unique field ecology program studying wildfire in one of California's most beautiful coastal habitats.

internships.title: SCA Bay Area Urban Green Program
internships.description: Paid conservation jobs for Bay Area teens and young adults focused on parks restoration, environmental stewardship, and climate resilience. Work includes trail maintenance, habitat restoration, invasive species removal, and urban greening projects.
internships.whyApply: Get paid for hands-on conservation work in Bay Area parks — builds real environmental stewardship skills.

internships.title: LLNL High School Student Internship
internships.description: Summer internship at one of the nation's premier national laboratories. High school students work on engineering projects including vacuum technology, electronics, machining, prototyping, and robotics alongside world-class scientists and engineers.
internships.whyApply: Paid internship at a Department of Energy national lab — cutting-edge engineering projects with top researchers.

internships.title: San Jose Youth Climate Corps
internships.company: San Jose Conservation Corps + Charter School
internships.description: Paid summer internship for San Jose high school students passionate about environmental action. Youth work on climate resilience projects including urban tree planting, water conservation, solar energy installations, and community sustainability education.
internships.whyApply: Paid climate action work in Silicon Valley — tangible environmental impact with career-building skills.

internships.title: OPALS High School Research Internship
internships.company: UC San Diego Institute for Engineering in Medicine
internships.description: Pre-college STEM research internship at UC San Diego. Students work alongside researchers on biomedical engineering projects, gaining hands-on lab experience and scientific presentation skills.
internships.whyApply: Work on real biomedical engineering research at a top-tier UC research university.

internships.title: Heithoff-Brody High School Summer Scholars Program
internships.description: Elite 8-week summer research program at the world-renowned Salk Institute. High school students are embedded in research labs working on projects in molecular biology, genetics, neuroscience, and plant biology alongside leading scientists.
internships.whyApply: One of the most prestigious high school research programs in the country — Salk is a Nobel Prize-producing institute.

internships.title: REHS — Research Experience for High School Students
internships.company: San Diego Supercomputer Center (SDSC), UC San Diego
internships.description: Volunteer internship at the San Diego Supercomputer Center. Students work on computational research projects involving data science, machine learning, bioinformatics, or scientific visualization under mentorship from SDSC researchers.
internships.whyApply: Gain hands-on experience in supercomputing and data science at one of the nation's premier research computing centers.

internships.title: SMUD High School Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Paid 6-week summer internship at California's largest community-owned electric utility. High school juniors and seniors rotate through departments including engineering, IT, customer service, and renewable energy. Includes professional development workshops and mentorship.
internships.whyApply: Paid internship at a major utility company with exposure to clean energy careers — 55 spots available each summer.

internships.title: SHTEM Summer Research Internship
internships.description: Summer internship for high schoolers and community college students at Stanford focused on STEM research. Students engage in hands-on projects in areas such as data compression, machine learning, signal processing, and computational imaging.
internships.whyApply: Conduct real research at Stanford University with faculty mentorship — exceptional college application credential.

internships.title: Oakland Zoo Teen Summer Internship
internships.description: Summer internship for teens entering grades 10-12. Interns work alongside zookeepers and educators learning animal husbandry, conservation education, and guest engagement. Includes behind-the-scenes access to animal care facilities.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on animal care and conservation education experience at a respected Bay Area zoo.

internships.title: Aquarium of the Pacific VolunTEEN Program
internships.description: 10-week structured volunteer program for teens ages 14-16. Participants learn marine biology, exhibit interpretation, and public communication skills through 80 hours of hands-on experience with aquarium visitors and marine habitats.
internships.whyApply: Outstanding marine science volunteer experience — competitive selection process builds your application profile.

internships.title: Sacramento Zoo Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer program for teens at the Sacramento Zoo. Volunteers assist with animal enrichment, guest education, conservation programming, and special events while learning about wildlife biology and zoo management.
internships.whyApply: Great entry-level wildlife and veterinary experience in the Sacramento region.

internships.title: KP LAUNCH High School Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internship for high school students ages 16-19 in Northern California Kaiser Permanente service areas. Interns participate in departmental projects, weekly professional development workshops, and mentorship sessions while earning $24/hour.
internships.whyApply: One of the highest-paying high school internships in California with real healthcare industry exposure.

internships.title: UC Berkeley Center for Youth Development through Law — Summer Program
internships.description: Summer program providing local disadvantaged high school students with instruction in law on the Berkeley campus, life skills workshops, and law-related internship placements. Students gain exposure to legal careers through classroom learning and hands-on experience.
internships.whyApply: Learn law from UC Berkeley professors and gain a real legal internship placement — designed for underserved students.

internships.title: ACLU of Southern California Student Internship
internships.description: Internship at the ACLU SoCal working on civil rights and liberties cases. High school and college students assist with legal research, advocacy campaigns, community outreach, and policy analysis on issues including immigrant rights, criminal justice reform, and free speech.
internships.whyApply: Work on real civil rights cases at one of the most influential legal advocacy organizations in the country.

internships.title: Fresno Building Healthy Communities Youth Internship
