internships.description: Paid internship for Fresno County high school sophomores and juniors. Youth interns work on community health, environmental justice, and youth advocacy projects, becoming change agents within their schools and neighborhoods.
internships.whyApply: Paid community advocacy work in California's Central Valley — empowers students to drive real neighborhood change.

internships.title: Career Exploration Experience (CLAOC)
internships.company: CEO Leadership Alliance Orange County / Brighter Futures OC
internships.description: 6-week hybrid career exploration program connecting Orange County high school students with business leaders. Students attend workshops, site visits to major companies, and networking events covering industries from tech and healthcare to finance and marketing.
internships.whyApply: Network directly with Orange County CEOs and executives — excellent for students exploring business careers.

internships.title: Lawrence Hall of Science Teen Volunteer Program
internships.company: Lawrence Hall of Science, UC Berkeley
internships.description: Teen volunteers at UC Berkeley's Lawrence Hall of Science facilitate hands-on STEM activities for museum visitors, lead science demonstrations, and assist with educational programs. Volunteers build public speaking, teaching, and science communication skills.
internships.whyApply: Build teaching and science communication skills at UC Berkeley's iconic public science center.

internships.title: CDFA High School Agricultural Internship
internships.company: California Department of Food and Agriculture
internships.description: State government internship at CDFA for high school and college students interested in agriculture, food safety, and environmental policy. Interns gain exposure to agricultural regulation, pest prevention, dairy and livestock programs, and food safety inspection.
internships.whyApply: Unique exposure to California's massive agricultural industry through the state's official food and agriculture department.

internships.title: West Sacramento Home Run Summer Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internships for Washington Unified School District high school students. Interns are placed across city departments and local organizations gaining real-world work experience in fields ranging from public works and parks to administration and recreation.
internships.whyApply: Full-time paid summer work experience through a well-organized city youth employment program.

internships.title: SF Youth Connect High School Internship Program
internships.company: SF Youth Connect / San Francisco Recreation and Parks
internships.description: Paid internship program placing San Francisco high school students with local nonprofit organizations and city agencies. Includes pre-employment training, ongoing mentorship, and professional development workshops throughout the school year.
internships.whyApply: Paid year-round nonprofit internship with structured mentorship — excellent for SF students passionate about community service.

internships.title: County of Santa Clara Student Internship Program
internships.description: County government internship pairing high school and college students with positions across Santa Clara County agencies including public health, social services, parks and recreation, and IT. Tasks and lengths vary by department.
internships.whyApply: Work inside Silicon Valley's county government — great exposure to public service careers in the heart of the tech region.

internships.title: Make-A-Wish Orange County High School Summer Internship
internships.description: Summer internship at Make-A-Wish OC/IE where high school students support wish-granting operations, fundraising events, community outreach, and marketing. Students gain nonprofit management experience while helping grant wishes for children with critical illnesses.
internships.whyApply: Meaningful nonprofit work at one of the most beloved charitable organizations — strong community service credential.

internships.title: LA Zoo Teen Council for Conservation
internships.company: Los Angeles Zoo and Botanical Gardens
internships.description: Nine-month program for LA high school students focused on conservation, environmental justice, and climate action. Up to 30 members engage in a blend of virtual and in-person meetings, field trips, and conservation projects throughout the school year.
internships.whyApply: Deep-dive conservation leadership program at LA's premier zoo — limited to 30 students, building a powerful peer network.

internships.title: Evodyne Robotics High School Internship
internships.description: Hands-on robotics engineering internship for high school students. Interns work on projects involving CAD design, electronics, 3D printing, and programming robotic systems. Students gain practical engineering skills while contributing to real robotics projects.
internships.whyApply: Build real robots with professional tools — CAD, 3D printing, and programming in a startup environment.

internships.title: TECH360 Intro to AI Summer Bootcamp
internships.description: Intensive 3-week hybrid bootcamp for rising juniors and seniors exploring AI and web development. Students learn HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, machine learning concepts, and predictive modeling. Culminates with a group project pitching an AI-powered tool to mock clients.
internships.whyApply: Free AI bootcamp with a $500 stipend — no prior coding experience required.

internships.title: UC Merced High School Computer Science Internship
internships.description: 12-week virtual research internship where 4 selected students work directly with a CS professor building real-world software systems. Students learn responsible research conduct, ethical standards, and gain hands-on software development experience. Particularly seeks Central Valley applicants.
internships.whyApply: Direct mentorship from a UC professor on real software research — free and fully virtual.

internships.title: Computer Science Immersion Program (C-SIP)
internships.description: 9-session Saturday afternoon program where high school students explore computer science career pathways. Hear presentations from professionals in web design, software development, and UX design. Gain exposure to real industry roles and build a network in tech.
internships.whyApply: Low time commitment way to explore CS careers with industry professionals.

internships.title: LA County Bloomberg Arts Internship (BAI)
internships.company: LA County Department of Arts and Culture
internships.description: 8-week paid summer internship connecting rising public high school seniors with cultural institutions across LA County. Includes professional development workshops, college preparation support, dedicated mentorship, and educational field trips to arts organizations. Supported by Bloomberg Philanthropies.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious paid arts internship at $27.81/hr with college prep and mentorship included.

internships.title: LACMA Art & Tech Teen Internship
internships.company: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
internships.description: 6-month paid residency for high school juniors and seniors at LACMA. Interns work on specialized art and technology projects for the Education Community Programs department, gaining hands-on museum experience. Designed for students from historically marginalized communities interested in museum careers.
internships.whyApply: Work at one of the largest art museums in the western US — paid position combining art and technology.

internships.title: Holocaust Museum LA High School Summer Internship
internships.description: Immersive summer program for high school students exploring Holocaust history, survivor testimony, and museum education. Participants engage with primary sources, art, media, and personal narratives while developing skills in research, public speaking, and historical analysis.
internships.whyApply: Powerful educational experience combining historical research, media, and museum skills.

internships.title: Scripps Research REACH Summer Internship
internships.description: 7-week paid summer research internship at Scripps Research for San Diego County high school students. Includes a mandatory 3-day boot camp followed by immersive lab research. Students work directly with scientists on cutting-edge biomedical research projects. Targets students from underrepresented backgrounds.
internships.whyApply: Paid research at a world-class research institute — exceptional for aspiring scientists.

internships.title: FACES for the Future at Rady Children's Hospital
internships.description: 3-year pathway program starting sophomore year that prepares high school students from underserved areas for healthcare careers. Includes clinical internships, health professional mentoring, healthcare environment exposure, and academic support. Students gain direct experience in a major children's hospital.
internships.whyApply: Multi-year healthcare pathway program at a top children's hospital — starts sophomore year.

internships.title: LegiSchool Civics Virtual Institute
internships.company: Sacramento State Center for California Studies
internships.description: Intensive 4-week online summer program exploring California policy issues. Students meet virtually Mon/Wed/Fri afternoons, develop leadership and professional skills, and learn how state government works. Open to all CA sophomores, juniors, and seniors.
internships.whyApply: Unique chance to learn CA state policy from Sacramento State experts — fully virtual and accessible statewide.

internships.title: California State Assembly District Office Internship
internships.description: Hands-on internship in a California Assembly member's district office. High school and college students experience daily legislative operations, constituent services, policy research, and community outreach. Positions available in district offices statewide.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to state government operations — available in district offices throughout California.

internships.title: CLA High School Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internship at one of the nation's largest accounting and professional services firms. High school students gain exposure to accounting, tax, advisory, and financial consulting through hands-on projects and mentorship from professionals. Available at CLA offices in California.
internships.whyApply: Paid exposure to professional accounting at a top-10 national firm — real client work experience.

internships.title: San Diego County Behavioral Health Student Internship (YEA Pilot)
internships.company: County of San Diego Health and Human Services
internships.description: Paid part-time (15 hrs/week) non-clinical internship in behavioral health services. High school, undergraduate, and graduate students gain experience in mental health program administration, community outreach, and public health systems within county government.
internships.whyApply: Paid government health internship — great for students interested in psychology or public health careers.

internships.title: Higher Ground Youth Mentor Internship
internships.description: Hands-on mentoring internship serving as a positive role model in afterschool programs for youth in underserved Orange County communities. Interns lead activities, tutor students, and develop leadership and communication skills while making a direct community impact.
internships.whyApply: Meaningful mentorship experience in Orange County — develop leadership while serving your community.

internships.title: San Diego Natural History Museum Teen Internship
internships.description: Unpaid internship for high school students age 16+ rotating through three science departments: Entomology, Paleontology, and Botany. Interns receive hands-on training in specimen handling, field collection, and scientific cataloging while working alongside museum researchers.
internships.whyApply: Rotate through three real science departments — rare behind-the-scenes museum research experience.

internships.title: USDA Pathways Internship Program — California
internships.description: Paid federal internship providing high school students hands-on experience in agriculture, food safety, natural resources, and rural development. Work alongside USDA scientists and professionals at California field offices. Flexible scheduling year-round or summer-only options.
internships.whyApply: Paid federal internship — excellent for students interested in agriculture, food science, or environmental careers.

internships.title: OC Public Libraries Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Volunteer program for teens at Orange County library branches. Students assist with summer reading programs, children's events, shelving, and community outreach. Earn community service hours while developing organizational and interpersonal skills.
internships.whyApply: Easy-access volunteer opportunity at library branches across Orange County — great for community service hours.

internships.title: Make-A-Wish Orange County & Inland Empire Marketing Internship
internships.description: Summer internship at Make-A-Wish assisting with marketing and communications. Interns develop skills in brand management, written and oral communications, social media, and event coordination while supporting the organization's mission of granting wishes to children with critical illnesses.
internships.whyApply: Build real marketing skills at a beloved nonprofit — flexible 8-10 hours per week.

internships.title: ACLU of Southern California Student Internship — Orange County
internships.description: Legal advocacy internship at ACLU SoCal's Orange County office in Santa Ana. Students assist with civil rights cases, legal research, community outreach, and advocacy campaigns. Gain direct exposure to civil liberties law and social justice work.
internships.whyApply: Work on real civil rights cases at one of America's most influential legal organizations.

internships.title: Orange County Government Student Internship Program
internships.company: County of Orange Human Resource Services
internships.description: Internships across multiple Orange County government departments including public works, health care, social services, and community resources. Students learn how local government functions through real project work impacting community development and public safety.
internships.whyApply: See how local government actually works — hands-on experience across multiple county departments.

internships.title: Meta Summer Academy — California
internships.description: 6-week paid summer internship at Meta headquarters giving high school students firsthand experience of the tech business world. Students explore different careers, learn financial literacy, build communication and networking skills, and get an introduction to coding.
internships.whyApply: Paid internship at a major tech company — explore business, tech, and financial literacy at Meta HQ.

internships.title: Children's Hospital Los Angeles Summer Volunteer Program
internships.description: 7-week summer volunteer program where high school students complete 112 service hours at one of the nation's top pediatric hospitals. Volunteers gain direct exposure to hospital operations, patient care environments, and healthcare career paths.
internships.whyApply: Gain 112 hours of hands-on hospital experience at a nationally ranked children's hospital.

internships.title: Cedars-Sinai High School Volunteer Program
internships.description: Year-round volunteer program for high school students interested in healthcare careers. Students assist clinical and administrative departments, gaining firsthand experience in a world-class hospital environment.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at one of the top hospitals in Los Angeles and explore diverse healthcare careers.

internships.title: UCLA Health High School Volunteer Program
internships.description: High school students volunteer in Patient Transport and other departments at UCLA Medical Center, assisting with patient discharges, transfers, and errands. Shifts available seven days a week between 8 AM and 8 PM.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at a top-ranked academic medical center with flexible scheduling throughout the year.

internships.title: Torrance Memorial High School Volunteer Program
internships.description: High school students ages 14-18 enrolled in 9th–11th grade volunteer in Escort Services, assisting with patient and staff errands throughout the hospital. Great introduction to hospital operations and healthcare careers.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on hospital volunteering open to students as young as 14 — great first healthcare experience.

internships.title: CAMP CHLA — Health Care Career Exploration
internships.description: Intensive health care career exploration program at Children's Hospital Los Angeles. High school students shadow medical professionals, participate in clinical simulations, and learn about diverse health career pathways in a pediatric hospital setting.
internships.whyApply: Immersive career exploration at a nationally ranked pediatric hospital — great for aspiring healthcare professionals.

internships.title: UC Berkeley embARC Summer Design Academy
internships.company: UC Berkeley College of Environmental Design
internships.description: Competitive summer design academy where high school students learn architecture, sustainable city planning, and urban design. Includes design assignments, 1-on-1 faculty reviews, field trips, and a design/build project for a community nonprofit.
internships.whyApply: Study architecture and urban design at UC Berkeley with faculty mentorship and real community impact projects.

internships.title: SCI-Arc High School Summer Program
internships.description: High school students explore architecture and design thinking at SCI-Arc's LA campus. The program introduces students to architectural design, fabrication, and creative problem-solving through hands-on studio projects.
internships.whyApply: Study architecture at one of the world's most innovative design schools in the heart of LA.

internships.title: USC Exploration of Architecture Summer Program
internships.description: Intensive summer program where high school students explore architecture through studio workshops, lectures, and field trips across Los Angeles. Students develop design portfolios and learn fundamentals of architectural practice.
internships.whyApply: Build an architecture portfolio at USC while exploring LA's iconic buildings and urban landscapes.

internships.title: Temecula Youth Entrepreneur Program
internships.description: Two-week summer entrepreneurship program for high school students ages 14–18 in Inland Southern California. Students learn business planning, marketing, financial literacy, and pitch presentation skills from local business leaders.
internships.whyApply: Free city-run entrepreneurship program — learn to build a business plan and pitch to real investors.

internships.title: San Diego Symphony High School Ambassadors
internships.company: San Diego Symphony / Jacobs Music Center
internships.description: Paid two-week summer internship combining professional development training with on-the-job experience at the San Diego Symphony. Ambassadors ages 16–18 earn $17.75/hour and receive complimentary concert tickets and networking opportunities.
internships.whyApply: Paid arts internship at a major symphony — build professional skills while experiencing the performing arts industry.

internships.title: SDSC Research Experience for High School Students (REHS)
internships.company: San Diego Supercomputer Center / UC San Diego
internships.description: 8-week summer volunteer research internship at the San Diego Supercomputer Center where high school students work alongside scientists on computational research projects in data science, machine learning, and high-performance computing.
internships.whyApply: Work on real computational research at one of the nation's premier supercomputing centers.

internships.title: Cal Poly SLO Architecture Summer Career Workshop
internships.description: 4-week summer architecture and environmental design workshop at Cal Poly SLO. High school students explore architectural design, structural engineering concepts, and sustainable building practices through hands-on studio projects.
internships.whyApply: Immersive architecture workshop at Cal Poly's renowned learn-by-doing program.

internships.title: Fresno EOC Youth Summer Employment Program
internships.description: Paid summer internship and employment program placing Fresno-area high school students in local businesses, nonprofits, and government agencies. Students gain real-world work experience while earning wages and developing professional skills.
internships.whyApply: Paid summer employment in Fresno's Central Valley — great for students seeking their first professional experience.

internships.title: Central California Health Career Connection Internship
internships.description: Paid summer health internship placing students in healthcare organizations throughout the Central Valley including Fresno, Stockton, Madera, Merced, and Bakersfield. Students explore health careers through hands-on clinical and administrative placements.
internships.whyApply: Paid health internship in the Central Valley — excellent pipeline to healthcare careers in underserved communities.

internships.title: UC Irvine Summer Healthcare Experience Program
internships.description: One-week intensive healthcare experience for high school students ages 16–18 entering junior or senior year. Students participate in shadowing, ultrasound workshops, simulator training, and learn about health career pathways at UC Irvine Medical Center.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on medical simulations and shadowing at a UC medical center — ideal for aspiring doctors and nurses.

internships.title: SFPUC YouthWorks Summer Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship for SF high school students at the San Francisco Public Utilities Commission. Interns work alongside city employees in departments including engineering, environmental science, communications, and public policy.
internships.whyApply: Paid city government internship combining environmental science with public service in San Francisco.

internships.title: SF Public Works Youth Outreach — Architecture Program
internships.company: San Francisco Department of Public Works
internships.description: Paid summer program where high school students learn architecture and landscape architecture through hands-on projects with SF Public Works professionals. Students explore urban design, public space planning, and civic architecture.
internships.whyApply: Paid architecture program with real city projects — learn how public spaces are designed and built.

internships.title: CA Speaker of the Assembly Student Internship Program
internships.description: High school and college students intern in California State Assembly offices in Sacramento or district offices statewide. Interns assist with constituent services, legislative research, policy analysis, and community outreach.
internships.whyApply: Work directly with California state legislators on real policy issues and constituent services.

internships.title: LegiSchool Real World Civics Internship
internships.company: Sacramento State / Center for California Studies
internships.description: High school sophomores, juniors, and seniors work with the California State Legislature through the LegiSchool Project. Students engage in public policy discussions, meet state leaders, and gain direct experience with the legislative process.
internships.whyApply: Engage directly with California's state government and learn how public policy is made.

internships.title: LA County AJCC Youth Internship & Apprenticeship Program
internships.description: LA County's America's Job Center of California connects youth ages 16–24 with paid internship and apprenticeship placements across diverse industries including manufacturing, technology, healthcare, and government services.
